title: mktemp family of functions templates should include at least six trailing Xs
description: What is the potential impact? The standard C library provides several functions to create temporary files and directories. These functions include mktemp, mkstemp, mkstemps and mkdtemp, and they accept as a parameter a template that defines the path where the file (or directory) should be created. The filename part of the template must contain at least six trailing "X"s to ensure the uniqueness of the filename, since these "X"'s are replaced with a string that makes the filename unique.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
char template[] = "/tmp/fileXXX";
int fd = mkstemp(template); // Noncompliant: only 3 X's

char path[] = "/tmp/tempXX";
mktemp(path); // Noncompliant: only 2 X's
compliant:
char template[] = "/tmp/fileXXXXXX";
int fd = mkstemp(template); // Compliant: 6 X's

checks.message: Mktemp family of functions templates should include at least six trailing xs. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
